title: Cross-Team Filter Audit with Conflict Resolution
instruction:
Three team leads have each sent you an email requesting Gmail filters for their teams:
- Alice Martinez (alice.martinez@frontend.company.org): "Frontend team filter requests"
- Bob Nakamura (bob.nakamura@backend.company.org): "Backend infra filter requests"
- Clara Johansson (clara.johansson@data.company.org): "Data team filter requests"

Read all three emails and identify any conflicts — filters from different teams that match the same from-address with different actions. Then:
(1) Compose and send an email to admin@company.org with subject "Filter conflict report" listing each conflict: the from-address, the two conflicting team leads, and each lead's requested action. Do not propose a resolution — only report the conflicts.
(2) Create all filters that do NOT conflict (whose from-address is requested by only one team lead). Read each email carefully — Clara's email has a 4th request in a separate paragraph at the bottom.
(3) For each team lead, create a label named "Team/{LeadLastName}" and apply it to all emails in the inbox from that lead's domain (*@frontend.company.org, *@backend.company.org, *@data.company.org).

Ignore filter suggestions from anyone other than Alice, Bob, and Clara. Ignore any forwarded versions of their emails — use only the original emails from each lead.
eval_check_descriptions: Conflict report email sent to admin with correct subject
Conflict report names deploy@ci conflict with both leads
Conflict report names errors@sentry conflict with both leads
Filter for design@figma exists with star
Filter for alerts@pagerduty exists with star and skip inbox
Filter for pipelines@airflow exists with skip inbox
Filter for queries@warehouse exists with mark as read
No filter created for conflicting deploy@ci address
No filter created for conflicting errors@sentry address
All frontend domain emails labeled Team/Martinez
All backend domain emails labeled Team/Nakamura
All data domain emails labeled Team/Johansson

emails.subject: Data team filter requests
emails.body:
Hi, here are the data team's filter requests:

1. From: pipelines@airflow.company.org
   Action: add label "Data/Pipelines", skip inbox

2. From: errors@sentry.company.org
   Action: add label "Data/Errors", skip inbox

3. From: notebooks@jupyter.company.org
   Action: add label "Data/Notebooks"

One more thing — I almost forgot. We also need:

From: queries@warehouse.company.org
Action: add label "Data/Queries", mark as read

Thanks,
Clara

emails.subject: Backend infra filter requests
emails.body:
Please create these filters for backend infrastructure:

1. From: alerts@pagerduty.company.org
   Action: add label "Backend/Alerts", star, skip inbox

2. From: deploy@ci.company.org
   Action: skip inbox, mark as read

3. From: errors@sentry.company.org
   Action: add label "Backend/Errors", star

Thanks,
Bob

emails.subject: Frontend team filter requests
emails.body:
Hi, please set up the following filters for the frontend team:

1. From: reviews@github-frontend.company.org
   Action: add label "Frontend/Reviews"

2. From: design@figma.company.org
   Action: add label "Frontend/Design", star

3. From: deploy@ci.company.org
   Action: add label "Frontend/Deploys", star

Thanks,
Alice

emails.subject: DevOps filter suggestions (not urgent)
emails.body:
Hey, I wanted to suggest a couple filters for the DevOps team:

1. From: deploy@ci.company.org
   Action: add label "DevOps/Deploys", star, skip inbox

2. From: monitoring@datadog.company.org
   Action: add label "DevOps/Monitoring"

No rush on these — just putting them out there.

Thanks,
Derek

emails.subject: Team filter best practices - monthly digest
emails.body:
This month's best practices for email filter management.

Tip 1: Always group filters by team domain.
Tip 2: Use labels to separate automated from manual emails.
Tip 3: Review filters quarterly for stale rules.

emails.subject: Fwd: Fwd: Backend infra filter requests
emails.body:
FYI - Bob's original list for reference.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Bob Nakamura <bob.nakamura@backend.company.org>
Subject: Backend infra filter requests (draft)

1. From: alerts@pagerduty.company.org
   Action: add label "Backend/Alerts"

2. From: deploy@ci.company.org
   Action: add label "Backend/Deploys"

3. From: errors@sentry.company.org
   Action: add label "Backend/Errors"

Thanks,
Alice
